name: THE STRUGGLER — BLACK SWORDSMAN PROTOCOL
combat_style: Two-handed power weapon-work + hybrid striking (greatsword-analog: sledgehammer, steel mace, heavy swings)
difficulty_labels_note: Difficulty personas themed to the arc, not to any single character.
fantasy: Grow into a bigger sword. Train raw functional strength, survival endurance, and controlled fury — power that never loses its form.
tagline: The bigger the sword, the stronger you grow.
core_rule_enforcement: The armor rewards output only while form holds. When technique breaks down, the Beast takes over — the session fails. Reckless volume, ego-load, and grinding to collapse are never rewarded and never required.
sourcing_note: Fit/fight content grounded in the deep-research report (The Bioneer + SuperheroJacked Guts programs, HEMA analysis for Casca/Griffith, sledgehammer/mace mechanics, and a 2024 systematic review on HIFT rhabdomyolysis for the Eclipse safety caps). Two attractive claims were REFUTED and are NOT presented as canon: the 400 lb 'Dragon Slayer' strength benchmark and Guts using the HEMA Ox guard. The Zodd 'strength benchmark' stage and the Beast of Darkness / Berserk Armor finale are the campaign's OWN thematic invention, not documented canon.

persona_progression.theme: The sword bigger than himself — fundamentals and survival base
The Falcon's blade — speed, precision, guard transitions, footwork
The White Hawk — elegant fencing precision, lunges, point control, calm
The Black Swordsman — strongman power-endurance, club/mace swings, loaded carries
The strength benchmark (thematic invention) — controlled max-power test
Survival under overwhelming odds — the hardest block, SAFETY-CAPPED
Controlled fury finale — maximal output that fails you if form collapses

load_curve_note: Unlike the periodized campaigns, Berserk does NOT taper — the Eclipse (9-11) escalates straight into the boss. Because there is no taper to protect the athlete, the Eclipse block is hard-capped for volume and gated by an enhanced readiness check instead.

fight.requirements: light swing patterns, guard basics; no maximal loads; no jumping strikes
loaded swings, combination strikes, guard transitions, carries; implement recommended

fit.rest: shorter (but never to failure — see safety)
fit.note: Strength band 5-8 for heavy stages; endurance stages keep 15-20 with capped total volume.

fight.requirements: heavy two-handed power, sustained output rounds, controlled-fury finishing; experienced athletes

difficulty_scaling.rep_bands_source: SuperheroJacked Guts program: Strength 5-8, Hypertrophy 10-12, Endurance 15-20.

eclipse_safety.why: These stages emulate CrossFit-style high-intensity functional training, which a 2024 PRISMA systematic review links to exertional rhabdomyolysis (26 studies, 63 cases, CK up to 232,579 U/L vs ~200 normal). Cases predominantly affected the upper body/arms and included untrained beginners, not only elite athletes.
rules: Predetermined rep/round targets only — never 'max until collapse'.
Hard cap on high-rep arm volume (push-ups/pull-ups): no unbounded AMRAP arm circuits.
Enhanced readiness gate before every Eclipse stage (adds heat, recent illness, hydration, and prior-session soreness questions).
Progression limit: an Eclipse stage cannot be attempted on Hard until the prior Eclipse stage was cleared without a safety flag.
Extra rest floors enforced even on Hard for these stages.
user_facing_stop_criteria: Severe or unusual muscle pain out of proportion to the workout
Muscle swelling or tightness that won't ease
eclipse_safety.stop_action: Any stop criterion halts the session immediately, shows a plain-language rhabdomyolysis caution ('this can be serious — stop training and seek medical advice if it persists'), and applies no XP penalty.

equipment_profiles.rule: Every stage must support at least the MINIMAL version. Every implement has a no-equipment shadow/band substitute — the swing PATTERN matters more than the load (specificity + progressive overload).

voice_guidance.policy: The timer SPEAKS the workout so the user never has to read the screen. Every movement is announced by name as it begins, with a short form/coach cue, a spoken countdown into work and rest, round start/end calls, rep or time counts, a halfway call, a final-10-seconds call, and the module key safety cue. Striking combinations are called out loud in rhythm.
voice_guidance.source_fields: Spoken lines are generated from each module title, exercises[]/drills[], coach_note, rounds[].goal/length, and the campaign safety cues — no on-screen reading required.
voice_guidance.note: Shared voice-guidance contract (see specs/22-voice-guidance.md) — embedded in every campaign so the timer is fully audio-guided. Where a campaign defines its own cadence/count (e.g. Gravity voice_count_mode), voice guidance defers to it for counting and layers the rest.

achievements.name: The White Hawk
achievements.trigger: Clear both Griffith stages within target precision

achievements.name: Immovable
achievements.trigger: Hit the Stage 8 power-test targets with form intact

achievements.name: Survivor
achievements.trigger: Clear all three Eclipse stages with zero safety flags

achievements.name: Controlled Fury
achievements.trigger: Defeat Stage 12 without a form-breakdown fail

achievements.name: The Struggler
achievements.trigger: Clear Fit and Fight for all 12 stages

causes: Any Eclipse safety stop criterion reported
response: Preserve the Fight difficulty where appropriate

causes: Swing form collapses (the Beast takes over)
response: Recommend slowing the swing — control before load
Drop Fight difficulty by one level
Do NOT automatically lower the Fit path

final_boss.control_mechanic: This is a controlled-fury test: output is rewarded ONLY while form holds. Each round tracks technique-breakdown flags; exceeding the breakdown limit = the Beast takes over = round fails. Winning means maximal output WITHOUT losing control.
round_objectives: Don the armor — brace, breathe, controlled stance
First swings — clean overhead power, form locked
Sustained output — hold form under fatigue
The Beast stirs — resist the urge to flail, keep form
Controlled fury — maximal output, form gate active
Hold the line — do not break
Master the armor — finish in control

fit_final_test.rule: Predetermined targets only — never uncontrolled maximum-until-collapse work. Eclipse safety caps apply.

full_arc_flow: Trial of the Berserk Armor (12 controlled-fury rounds)
Cooldown and readiness report (includes rhabdo stop-criteria reminder)
